body.title: Security: restrict agent_halt() to rack-admin callers only
body.result: Already implemented: skeleton.py agent_halt() MCP tool enforces rack-admin allowlist (line 195) and proof token validation (line 213) before dispatching to HaltRegistry — unauthorized callers receive DENY with governance log entry
body.description:
Gap surfaced by T-murderbot-security-review. T-agent-kill-switch exposes agent_halt(agent_id, reason) as an MCP tool but does not restrict which callers may invoke it. If available to all registered agents including ContainerShim-tier (untrusted), any agent can halt Igor, CC, or Granny — a critical DoS capability.

The halt tool must be restricted to rack-admin callers. Define what 'rack-admin' means in the provenance model (e.g., a declared admin agent_id list in config, or a separate admin token tier). Untrusted and ContainerShim-tier agents must receive a permission-denied error on agent_halt().

**Affected files:** devices/policy/ (authorization rule for agent_halt), unseen_university/skeleton/ (halt MCP tool — add caller check)
**Scope boundary:** IN — caller authorization check on agent_halt() and agent_resume(); definition of admin-tier callers; denial logged to decision bill of materials; OUT — multi-admin delegation, audit of who defined the admin list
**Completion criteria:** a ContainerShim-tier agent calling agent_halt('igor', 'test') receives permission-denied; a rack-admin caller succeeds; denial logged; test covers both cases
**Akien approval note (2026-06-02):** agent_halt() must cut inference to that device until explicitly unhalted — not just log the call. All our callers are internal; breaking uncredentialed callers is intentional and correct.
